description_long: This bungalow features a broad, low-pitched roof with deep overhangs and a prominent front gable, characteristic of traditional Japanese or East Asian architectural influences. The roof is clad in dark green tiles or metal sheeting, with a secondary gabled section extending from the main structure, likely covering a porch or entryway. The building sits on a series of visible supports or stilts, elevating it slightly above the ground. The exterior walls are finished in a muted brown, contrasting with the dark roof, and the structure appears to be single-storied. The front section includes an open, covered area supported by posts, suggesting a sheltered porch or veranda. Windows are not distinctly visible, indicating either minimal fenestration or traditional shoji-style panels.
description: A single-story bungalow with a wide, dark green gabled roof, brown exterior walls, and a prominent covered front porch supported by posts.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main gabled roof, the extended covered porch, and supporting posts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the roof, the secondary gable, and the building's elevated base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full width of the main roof and the absence of a porch or entry features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, with the secondary gable and supporting posts visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: minimal or concealed, likely traditional panel arrangement
